content.label: Identify sqrt(a^2 - x^2) -> x = a sin theta
content.applies_when: the integrand has the form sqrt(a^2 - x^2) with a = 3, which calls for the sine substitution x = a sin theta

content.meaning: let x = 3 sin theta so that dx = 3 cos theta dtheta and sqrt(9 - x^2) = 3 cos theta

content.concept: sin theta = x/3, cos theta = sqrt(9 - x^2)/3
content.meaning: recover the triangle: sin theta = x/3, cos theta = sqrt(9 - x^2)/3, theta = arcsin(x/3)

content.label: Substitute and simplify
content.action: substitute x = 3 sin theta to get 9 integral cos^2 theta dtheta
content.purpose: clear the radical and reduce to a power of cosine

content.label: Power-reduce and integrate
content.action: power-reduce: 9 integral (1 + cos 2theta)/2 dtheta = (9/2)(theta + sin(2theta)/2) = (9/2) theta + (9/2) sin theta cos theta
content.purpose: carry out the theta-integral using the double angle

content.label: Convert back from theta to x
content.action: use sin theta = x/3, cos theta = sqrt(9 - x^2)/3, theta = arcsin(x/3): (x/2) sqrt(9 - x^2) + (9/2) arcsin(x/3) + C
content.purpose: return the antiderivative to the original variable x
